records.ety: [AS. sp<?/d success, swiftness, from sp<?/wan to succeed; akin to D. spoedd, OHG. spuot success, spuot to succees, Skr. sphā to increase, grow fat. √170b.]

defs: Prosperity in an undertaking; favorable issue; success.
quotes: O Lord God of my master Abraham, I pray thee, send me good speed this day.

defs: The act or state of moving swiftly; swiftness; velocity; rapidly; rate of motion; dispatch; as, the speed a horse or a vessel.
quotes: Speed, to describe whose swiftness number fails.
notes: ☞ In kinematics, speedis sometimes used to denote the amount of velocity without regard to direction of motion, while velocity is not regarded as known unless both the direction and the amount are known.

defs: One who, or that which, causes or promotes speed or success.

records.ety: [AS. spēdan, fr. spēd, n.; akin to D. spoeden, G. sich sputen. See Speed, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sped (spd), Speeded; p. pr. & vb. n. Speeding.]

quotes: To warn him now he is too farre sped.

defs: To experience in going; to have any condition, good or ill; to fare.
quotes: Ships heretofore in seas like fishes sped; The mightiest still upon the smallest fed.

defs: To fare well; to have success; to prosper.
quotes: Save London, and send true lawyers their meed! For whoso wants money with them shall not speed!
I told ye then he should prevail, and speed On his bad errand.

defs: To make haste; to move with celerity.
quotes: I have speeded hither with the very extremest inch of possibility.

defs: To cause to be successful, or to prosper; hence, to aid; to favor.
quotes: With rising gales that speed their happy flight.

defs: To cause to make haste; to dispatch with celerity; to drive at full speed; hence, to hasten; to hurry.
quotes: He sped him thence home to his habitation.

defs: To hasten to a conclusion; to expedite.
quotes: Judicial acts . . . are sped in open court at the instance of one or both of the parties.

defs: To hurry to destruction; to put an end to; to ruin; to undo.
quotes: A dire dilemma! either way I 'm sped. If foes, they write, if friends, they read, me dead.

defs: To wish success or god fortune to, in any undertaking, especially in setting out upon a journey.
quotes: Welcome the coming, speed the parting guest.
